title: Introduction to Java & Your First Program
learningObjectives: Understand what Java is and why it's popular
Know the difference between JDK, JRE, and JVM
Write, compile, and run your first Java program
Understand Java program structure and syntax

steps.title: What is Java & How it Works
steps.description: Read through today's theory covering Java history, JDK/JRE/JVM architecture, and your first program.

questions.question: Who created Java and in which year?
What does WORA stand for and what does it mean?
What is the difference between JDK, JRE, and JVM?
What is bytecode? Why is it important?
Why does Java need both a compiler AND an interpreter?
What is the role of the Garbage Collector in JVM?
Why must the filename match the public class name?
What is the purpose of the main method in Java?
What does 'public static void main(String[] args)' mean — explain each keyword?
What is the difference between print(), println(), and printf()?

challenges.title: Print I Love Java
challenges.description: Write a program to print "I love Java" 5 times (use 5 println statements).

testCases.expectedOutput:
I love Java
I love Java
I love Java
I love Java
I love Java
testCases.description: Should print 'I love Java' exactly 5 times

hints: Use System.out.println("I love Java"); five times

challenges.title: Triangle Pattern
challenges.description:
Write a program to print a triangle pattern using * and println:
```
*
**
***
****
*****
```
hints: Use System.out.println("*"); for the first line
Each subsequent line adds one more * character

challenges.title: Formatted Introduction
challenges.description:
Write a program that uses printf to display:
```
Student: Chandu
Age: 22 years
GPA: 8.5
```
hints: Use %d for integers and %.1f for doubles

challenges.title: Box Pattern
challenges.description:
Write a program that prints a 5x5 box made of * characters:
```
*****
*   *
*   *
*   *
*****
```
hints: Middle rows have * at start and end with spaces in between

challenges.title: Receipt Printer
challenges.description:
Write a program that prints a simple receipt with item names and prices using printf:
```
--- RECEIPT ---
Item          Price
Apple         $1.50
Bread         $2.75
Milk          $3.20
--- TOTAL: $7.45 ---
```

testCases.expectedOutput:
--- RECEIPT ---
Item          Price
Apple         $1.50
Bread         $2.75
Milk          $3.20
--- TOTAL: $7.45 ---

hints: Use printf with %-14s for left-aligned strings
Use $%.2f for prices with 2 decimal places

exercises.title: Missing Semicolon
exercises.hint: Every statement in Java must end with a semicolon ;

exercises.title: Case Sensitivity — Method Name
exercises.hint: Java is case-sensitive. The entry point method has a specific casing — main, not Main.

exercises.title: Case Sensitivity — println
exercises.hint: Check the casing of println — Java is case-sensitive.

exercises.title: Missing Array Brackets
exercises.hint: The main method parameter should be an array of Strings: String[] args

exercises.title: Naming Convention
exercises.hint: Class names in Java should start with an uppercase letter (PascalCase).
